regime_fall.current_status: The Iranian regime under Mojtaba Khamenei faces escalating military pressure from sustained US-Israeli strikes since late February, with comprehensive attacks on March 15-19 targeting military infrastructure across 26 provinces and killing senior officials. Despite defiant rhetoric and continued asymmetric retaliation through missile and drone attacks, Iran shows signs of internal fracturing, extended communication blackouts, and isolated diplomatic positioning as the US deploys additional amphibious forces and rejects ceasefire negotiations.
regime_fall.last_major_event: US and Israeli forces executed comprehensive multi-day strikes on March 15-19, 2026, targeting Iranian military command structures, air defense systems, missile production, IRGC bases, and energy infrastructure across 26 provinces, killing senior officials including Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib, Security Chief Ali Larijani, and Basij Chief Gholamreza Soleimani, while Brent crude surged above $108 per barrel and additional US amphibious forces were deployed to the region.
